profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.subtitle: The working-mode entry point. A profile switches model, provider, network, MCP, and safety policy together.
profiles.createHint: A diff is generated first. The config is backed up and written only after confirmation.
profiles.mcpSubsection: MCP tools referenced by this profile

providers.title: Providers
providers.subtitle: Manage model providers written into ~/.codex/config.toml. Subscription, official API, compatible API, and local gateway stay separated to avoid auth conflicts.
providers.createHint: A diff is generated first. The config is backed up and written only after confirmation. Enter an environment variable name, never a raw API key.
providers.networkSubsection: Network route attached to this provider

models.title: Model Config
models.subtitle: Codex Box setup: enter model name, endpoint URL, and API key, then choose which models appear in the Codex picker.
models.catalogSourceHint: This file records which models appear in the Codex picker. Adding a model writes here; publishing to Codex writes this path into ~/.codex/config.toml.
models.selectModelHint: Details now follow the model you click instead of defaulting to the first row, so MiniMax, GPT, and other upstreams stay visually separated.
models.routerHint: Publish the current model catalog into the Codex config, then let the Codex App enter the local proxy and route each model ID to its real API service.
models.publishHint: This writes the model catalog, Codex config, and picker cache. Restart the Codex App afterward, then choose the model from the picker.
models.officialAuthNoticeTitle: Combined mode depends on the official Codex sign-in
models.officialAuthNoticeBody: After publishing, subscription models and third-party models enter the local proxy through the same Codex picker entry. If the Codex App refresh token is stale, the official sign-in check may block the request before it reaches MiniMax, DeepSeek, or another upstream; the Logs page shows the actual model and auth source that were hit.
models.codexLinkReadyDetail: Current model={{model}} / provider={{provider}}; the Codex App will read the picker from the Codex Box catalog.
models.codexLinkMissingCatalog: config.toml does not point at ~/.codex/codex-box/custom_model_catalog.json, so the Codex App picker will not reliably read this catalog.
models.codexLinkWrongProvider: Current model_provider={{provider}} is not using the Codex Box published config yet. Save and publish to Codex first.
models.codexLinkUnknownDetail: Real status has not been read yet. Refresh or check Advanced Diagnostics in Settings.
models.visionHint: Enable Vision Bridge for text-only models: screenshots are described by a vision model before being sent to the selected model.
models.visionEnable: Enable vision fallback for this model

form.aliasHint: Supports provider:model, display=backend, and display->backend formats.
form.responsesHint: Recommended: passthrough when the provider supports Responses API, such as MiniMax.
form.chatHint: Compatibility mode: use when the upstream only supports Chat Completions; Codex Box translates it back to Responses.
form.simpleHint: Fill this once. After saving, the model appears in the Codex picker.
form.secretHint: Plain keys are not written to providers.json; Codex Box writes a $ENV reference and injects it for this run.
form.restartAfterUpdate: Remind me to restart Codex Desktop after updating

models.byokHintBody: Most users only need this page: add a model, enter its endpoint URL and API key, then choose whether it appears in the Codex picker. Advanced checks live in Settings.

modelRouter.title: Route Diagnostics Workspace
modelRouter.subtitle: Merge official subscription models, third-party APIs, and local models into one Codex picker, then route each model ID to the real upstream.

sources.title: API service sources
sources.help: Reads ~/.codex/codex-box/providers.json. These are real request destinations, not the models shown in the Codex picker.
sources.empty: No API service yet. Add a model in Models, or maintain services in API Services.

models.title: Picker models
models.help: Reads the Codex Box model catalog and merges the official default model view. Only visible models enter route tests and picker publishing.
models.empty: No visible model yet. Add and enable a model in Models.

routes.title: Route preview and publish
routes.help: Generate a publish diff before writing. Publishing writes providers.json, custom_model_catalog.json, config.toml, models_cache.json, and then attempts Codex Desktop picker refresh.
routes.empty: No publish route yet. Generate a preview first.
routes.noPreview: No pending preview. Click Generate preview to inspect the config writes.

test.title: Test and picker
test.help: Dry-run is the default and never calls a real upstream. The real upstream test is explicit and returns only status code and latency.
test.cdpReady: Codex Desktop exposes CDP port {{port}}, so the picker patch can be injected.
test.cdpMissing: Codex Desktop has no CDP port. If the picker still misses models, fully quit Codex Desktop and use Launch and unlock.
test.empty: No test result yet. Pick a model and run the dry-run test.

feedback.publishedAndUnlocked: Published {{routes}} routes and {{models}} models, then injected the picker patch.
feedback.publishedNeedsUnlock: Published {{routes}} routes and {{models}} models; picker still needs attention: {{message}}
feedback.noModel: No visible model is available for testing.

providerRoutes.title: API Services
providerRoutes.subtitle: Manage each model provider's endpoint URL and API key reference, such as MiniMax, OpenRouter, or OpenCode. Models choose one API service when sending requests.
providerRoutes.explainBody: An API service is not a model. It only stores where requests go and which environment variable supplies the key. Models that appear in the Codex picker are managed in Model Config.
providerRoutes.dataSourceLocal: Added from Models / added here / compatibility import
providerRoutes.dataSourceHint: Adding a model in Models also creates its API service. You can also add one here. Third-party configs are scanned only from Import and are never reused as live Codex Box services.
providerRoutes.emptyNotice: The API service list is empty. Add a service first.

codexRuntime.title: Codex Runtime
codexRuntime.subtitle: Read-only detection of Codex Desktop, Codex CLI, and ~/.codex/codex-box/. Codex Box does not modify Codex Desktop internals and does not start any external gateway process.
codexRuntime.readonlyHintBody: This page only shows install paths, file locations, and last-read times. Codex Box does not patch Codex Desktop internals; the real BYOK path is provided by the Codex Box Runtime 127.0.0.1 local proxy. After Codex App re-reads ~/.codex/config.toml, it fetches the merged model list from the local proxy.

codexBoxRuntime.title: Advanced Diagnostics
codexBoxRuntime.subtitle: Connect your configured models to Codex App. Start the local connection, then generate the config change; after Codex reloads config, the models appear in the picker.
codexBoxRuntime.previewHint: Start the proxy first, then click Preview to fetch the merged model list.
codexBoxRuntime.multirouterBody: Codex Box will preview and write providers.json, custom_model_catalog.json, config.toml, and models_cache.json so the Codex App picker can show native GPT and BYOK models together.
codexBoxRuntime.multirouterSkipped: {{count}} models have no usable upstream yet and will not be routed.

message.unknown: The current config and runtime state have not been loaded yet.
message.blocked: The chain has blocking issues, usually model, upstream endpoint, env var, or config write gaps.
message.proxyStopped: Codex config may already point local, but the 127.0.0.1 proxy is not running.
message.notEnabled: The local connection and model list are ready, but the config has not been written into Codex App yet.
message.needsAttention: The main path is connected, but warnings still need attention.
message.ready: Codex can use these models through the local connection.

actionHint.blocked: Review the routing checks below, fix failures first, then generate the connection config.
actionHint.proxyStopped: Start the local connection, then preview /v1/models.
actionHint.notEnabled: Generate the connection config, confirm the config.toml write, then restart Codex App.
actionHint.needsAttention: You can preview picker models now; warnings may affect some model calls.
actionHint.ready: Preview /v1/models or open Codex App after it reloads config and choose a model.

routeTest.title: Route Chain Test
routeTest.help: Simulate a Codex /v1/responses request with the current model catalog and verify resolver, protocol transform, and text-only image downgrade. The default dry-run does not call the real upstream.
routeTest.dryRun: This test checks local routing and protocol conversion only. It does not spend API quota.
routeTest.empty: Choose a model and run the dry-run to see whether it routes to the expected upstream.
routeTest.failed: Route dry-run failed. Review the steps.
routeTest.upstreamFailed: Real upstream test failed. Review the status code and steps.

desktopPicker.title: Codex App Picker
desktopPicker.help: After publishing the config, inject the merged model catalog into the Codex Desktop picker. Inject Only does not start Desktop; Launch & Unlock opens a CDP-enabled Codex Desktop only when Desktop is not running.
desktopPicker.launchDone: Codex Desktop launched and picker unlock completed.

import.title: Import External Config
import.help: The current model catalog references missing API services. Preview and import the OpenCodex config into the Codex Box home first, then publish to Codex.
import.providersEmpty: Codex Box API services are empty
import.previewReady: External config import preview is ready.
import.applyDone: External config imported into Codex Box and the publish preview is ready.
import.applyDoneNeedsConnect: External config imported into Codex Box, but the publish preview still needs attention.

advanced.title: Advanced tools and runtime details
advanced.hint: Conversation grouping, legacy base_url injection, and real upstream routing live here. Day to day, use the connection status and next step above; logs are in the left-side Logs page.

conversationProvider.title: Conversation Provider
conversationProvider.hint: This edits the model_provider name Codex App uses to group conversation history. Actual requests still go to the 127.0.0.1 local proxy; backend_provider in the model catalog decides the real upstream.
conversationProvider.requiresOpenaiAuth: Keep requires_openai_auth=true for Codex Desktop OpenAI login compatibility.

codexBoxRuntime.injectHint: Rewrites every non-subscription [model_providers.*].base_url to http://127.0.0.1:{port}/v1 and records the originals in ~/.codex/codex-box/inject-map.json. Codex App will route ALL third-party traffic through this proxy.
codexBoxRuntime.restoreHint: Reads ~/.codex/codex-box/inject-map.json and writes the original base_urls back into ~/.codex/config.toml. Use this when stopping the proxy or rolling back.
codexBoxRuntime.routeTableEmpty: No API services connected yet. Configure real endpoint sources in API Services first.
codexBoxRuntime.logsHint: Logs belong to runtime, not model config. This panel only shows redacted local runtime events.
codexBoxRuntime.sessionsHint: Sessions are separate from model config: models define available capability, sessions track runtime context.
codexBoxRuntime.safetyBody: Proxy only listens on 127.0.0.1 (never LAN). API keys are read from env vars at request time and never written to disk or logs. Every Inject/Restore goes through backup → diff → confirm → atomic write → rollback.

notice.injectOk: base_urls injected, restart Codex App to pick up new models
notice.activationPreviewOk: Connection config preview is ready. Review it before writing config.toml.
notice.activationApplyOk: Connection config written: {{model}} → {{provider}}. Restart Codex App to apply it.
notice.multirouterPreviewOk: Publish preview is ready. Confirm it before writing.

effectiveRouting.title: Effective routing chain
effectiveRouting.hint: Shows the path Codex App will actually use: Codex config → local connection → model config → real upstream.
effectiveRouting.empty: Effective routing has not been loaded yet.

simpleSteps.addModel: Enter model name, endpoint URL, and API key in Models.
simpleSteps.startLocal: Come back here to start the local connection and write the connection config.
simpleSteps.restartCodex: Restart Codex App, then choose the new model in the picker.

codexBoxRuntime.modelsEmpty: No available models yet. Add one in Models first.
codexBoxRuntime.activationBody: Codex Box will back up and update ~/.codex/config.toml so Codex App reads these models through the local connection.

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.subtitle: Grouped checks for config, provider, BYOK dir, network, MCP, and backup with a redacted report.

pickerUnlock.title: Picker Unlock
pickerUnlock.help: Injects the Codex Box model catalog into the Codex Desktop picker whitelist. Plain injection never starts Codex; launch-and-unlock opens a CDP-enabled Desktop only when Codex Desktop is not running.
pickerUnlock.launchDone: Codex Desktop launched and picker unlock completed.

historyUnify.title: Unify Conversation History
historyUnify.help: Merge old model_provider buckets in Codex local history DB and rollout JSONL into the current history provider. Before applying, Codex Box backs up SQLite, WAL/SHM, and touched JSONL files; writes are blocked while Codex is running.
historyUnify.codexRunning: Codex is running; safe write is blocked
historyUnify.previewBlocked: History unify cannot be applied safely yet.
historyUnify.applyDone: History unify completed and diagnostics refreshed.

logs.title: Logs
logs.subtitle: Inspect redacted events from the local connection, model picker, and request chain. Use this to see whether a request reached Codex Box and where it stopped.
logs.eventsHelp: Shows redacted local runtime events only. API keys, tokens, and full request bodies are never logged.
logs.sessionsHelp: Sessions help locate the current runtime context; they are not model configuration.
logs.sourceHelp: Codex Box reads local runtime state and emits redacted events. Request logs record entry, upstream response, and error causes without logging secrets.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: App-level settings for language, theme, safety policy, backup retention, log redaction, and BYOK behavior.
settings.runtimeDiagnosticsHint: Inspect the local proxy, Codex status, picker refresh, and import tools.
settings.routeDiagnosticsHint: Check model catalog, real upstreams, dry-run requests, and picker injection results.

draft.title: Draft
draft.desc: All edits enter a draft first

backup.title: Backup
backup.desc: Save the original config before write

sectionDesc.byok: BYOK write master switch, schema preservation, and visibility sync

language.label: Language
language.desc: Follow the current interface language and switch anytime.

theme.label: Theme
theme.desc: Keep the light Mac Dashboard direction. Dark mode will be added later.

startupCheck.label: Startup check
startupCheck.desc: Read config and refresh the Dashboard summary on launch.

requireDiff.label: Require diff before write
requireDiff.desc: Every save action must go through Config Diff first.

maskSecrets.label: Mask secrets
maskSecrets.desc: API keys, tokens, and passwords never appear in UI or logs.

confirmDanger.label: Confirm dangerous actions
confirmDanger.desc: Delete, rollback, and overwrite actions require confirmation.

backupFirst.label: Backup before write
backupFirst.desc: Create a backup record before changing config.

retention.label: Backup retention
retention.desc: Keep up to 50 backups by default; settings will be editable later.

rollback.label: Protected rollback
rollback.desc: Rollback also backs up the current state first.

redaction.label: Log redaction
redaction.desc: Diagnostics keep upstream, status, and latency summaries only.

maxSize.label: Log size limit
maxSize.desc: Rotate logs when they reach the configured limit.

exportReport.label: Export diagnostic report
exportReport.desc: Reports are redacted by default and safe to copy.

byokWriteEnabled.label: BYOK write master switch
byokWriteEnabled.desc: When off, Model Config and API Services are read-only and no writes hit ~/.codex/codex-box/.

byokSchemaPreserve.label: BYOK schema preservation
byokSchemaPreserve.desc: Preserve unknown fields when reading/writing ~/.codex/codex-box/*.json, so AITabby CLI stays compatible.

byokVisibilitySync.label: Visibility sync
byokVisibilitySync.desc: When toggling visibility on the Models page, sync the change into custom_model_catalog.json.

feedback.profileFormInvalid: Profile name, model, and provider are required.
feedback.profileExists: A Profile with this name already exists.
feedback.dangerBlocked: Dangerous action blocked. Real deletion must go through diff and backup.
feedback.providerFormInvalid: Fill in name, base_url, environment variable, and at least one model.
feedback.providerSecretRejected: Do not paste a raw secret here. Use an environment variable name such as OPENROUTER_API_KEY.
feedback.simpleModelFormInvalid: Enter model name, endpoint URL, and API key or env var.
feedback.simpleModelSaved: {{name}} was saved and added to the Codex picker model list.
feedback.configNotLoaded: Config is not loaded yet. Refresh and try again.
feedback.multirouterPreviewReady: Publish preview is ready. Confirm it before writing.
feedback.multirouterSyncedAndPickerUnlocked: Published to Codex: {{models}} models; Codex App picker refresh completed.
feedback.multirouterSyncedPickerPending: Published to Codex: {{models}} models; picker refresh still needs attention: {{message}}
feedback.modelsCacheRestorePreviewReady: Model cache restore preview is ready. Review the diff before restoring.
feedback.modelsCacheRestoreUnavailable: There is no Codex Box-owned model cache to restore.
feedback.conversationProviderSaved: Conversation Provider switched to {{name}}. Restart Codex App to apply it.
feedback.opencodexParseErrors: Action needed: one or more model providers saved an API key directly in the config file. Codex Box is not using those keys, but it still shows data that can be read safely. Change api_key to ${ENV_VAR} and keep the real key in your system environment:
feedback.modelsEmpty: The model catalog is empty. A Provider is only an API source; add model entries before Codex App can show models in its picker.
feedback.openLogs: Log entry is ready; real log reading will be wired later.
feedback.mcpToggled: {{name}} enabled state updated in frontend state.
feedback.writeBlocked: Safe save blocked: real writes require backup + diff + atomic write.
feedback.rollbackConfirm: Rollback entered confirmation state and will not overwrite config directly.
feedback.modelDeleteConfirm: Delete model "{{name}}"? This removes it from custom_model_catalog.json.
feedback.subscriptionModelDeleteBlocked: Subscription default models cannot be deleted. Hide them by turning off "Shown in picker".
feedback.modelDeletePending: Delete requested for {{name}}. Click Confirm below that model.

dashboard.statusOk: Codex config looks healthy · Last backup 2 minutes ago · {{time}}

systemStatus.title: System Status
systemStatus.desc: Only key signals needed to decide whether the app is usable.

entry.title: Key Entry Points
entry.desc: Only the main path stays here: configure models, inspect logs, adjust settings.

models.title: Configure Models
models.desc: Add API models and manage the picker

logs.title: Logs
logs.desc: Inspect local proxy and model call status
